2.description: Illustration of optimal tests. Panel A plots the optimal assignment of types־¸$\theta$to gradesz$z$under the optimal test described in Proposition1. Assignments are drawn for four example types־¸גˆˆ־¸0,־¸1,־¸2,־¸3$\theta \in \left\lbrace \theta _{0},\theta _{1},\theta _{2},\theta _{3}\right\rbrace $, and the associated expected payoffsvz$v\left(z\right)$are on the vertical axis. Thick arrows denote a deterministic grade that is assigned with probability one to a given type. Thin arrows denote a stochastic grade and are labeled with the associated probabilities. Panel B shows the implied distribution of optimal grades. The dashed curve is the cumulative prior distribution of types־¸$\theta$. The solid curve is the cumulative distribution of gradesz=E־¸|s$z=E\left[\theta |s\right]$implied by the optimal test described in Proposition1.
